channel.title: NYT > Style
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Armani Goes Back to the Archive
item.description: A new collection dusts off decades-old creations, plus a dispatch from a Brunello Cucinelli movie premiere and the Political Tie Tracker.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

item.title: A Rabbi Talks Torah on TikTok
item.description: Rabbi Shalom Landau has found a large new audience online, some of which isn’t Jewish. He sticks to ancient wisdom and hopes for the best.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Bernstein
item.media:credit: Jonah Markowitz for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rabbi Shalom Landau at his desk in the Williamsburg neighborhood of Brooklyn.

item.title: How the Brothers Behind Manhattan’s Aicon Art Gallery Found Themselves in Infinite Feuds
item.description: For a decade and a half, Prajit and Projjal Dutta have led the market for South Asian art in the United States. Now they rarely speak to each other, except through lawyers.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Bernstein
item.media:credit: Vincent Tullo for The New York Times

item.title: How Tim Cook’s Tech Uniform Helped Make Apple Trillions
item.description: The unassuming look of the outgoing Apple chief executive was an asset, as he navigated pop culture, the president and following Steve Jobs.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Jim Wilson/The New York Times
item.media:description: In his navy polos and dark jeans, Mr. Cook became a recognizable but unassuming figurehead of Apple. It may have been an asset for him to present as a kind of blank slate.

item.title: How ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ Red Carpet Looks Came Together
item.description: The press tour for “The Devil Wears Prada 2” has been a style spectacle for its stars and their stylists, who have relied on group chats, sweatpants and camaraderie to pull it off.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.media:credit: Mike Coppola/Getty Images

item.title: Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway and More Red Carpet Looks from “The Devil Wears Prada 2”
item.description: At a starry premiere at Lincoln Center, fashion industry figures reflected on just how much the original film got right.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann and Lanna Apisukh
item.media:description: Stars of the “The Devil Wears Prada 2,” Meryl Streep, Emily Blunt, Stanley Tucci and Anne Hathaway, pose at the film’s premiere at Lincoln Center in Manhattan.

item.title: Why Fashion Suddenly Loves Older Women
item.description: Representation of women of a certain age has never been higher. What’s going on?
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Queen Elizabeth II’s Off-Duty Wardrobe Goes on View at Buckingham Palace
item.description: A new exhibition takes a closer look at Queen Elizabeth II’s wardrobe of boots, barn coats, head scarves and tartan skirts.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi
item.category: Elizabeth II, Queen of Great Britain
item.media:credit: Tim Graham/Hulton Archive, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Head scarves, like the one Queen Elizabeth II wore to the Royal Windsor Horse Show in May 1968, were a pillar of her off-duty style.

item.title: She Found Out She Was Pregnant Just Before He Revealed an Affair
item.description: Rachel Sanders eventually came to terms with Jason Miller’s confession that he was involved with a mutual male friend. “I lost my marriage,” she said, “but I accepted Jason.”
item.dc:creator: Louise Rafkin

item.title: Make Way for the Investment Bank Influencers
item.description: Zoomers in finance are posting day-in-the-life videos on TikTok, alarming compliance officers. Should Wall Street reconsider its social media policies?
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark

item.title: Aldwyth, Ascetic Whose Artwork Reordered the World, Dies at 90
item.description: An austere lifestyle allowed her to make epic collages and Joseph Cornell-like contraptions that told stories about art history and her place in — and outside — it.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Green

item.title: N.F.L. Style Will Never Beat N.B.A. Style
item.description: Plus: some great Japanese designers and a final thought on the press tour for “The Devil Wears Prada 2.”
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher
item.media:credit: Kirby Lee/Imagn Images, via Reuters Connect

item.title: Inside the Time 100 Gala: Where Victoria and David Beckham Dine with MrBeast
item.description: Titans of influence filled the Deutsche Bank Center in Manhattan on Thursday night for an evening of selfies and self-congratulation.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Nina Westervelt
item.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times

item.title: Instead of the Bouquet, She Caught a Groomsman’s Attention
item.description: Krystal Miller and Brandon Manning’s first meeting at a wedding was a bit awkward. But by their second date, their chemistry was undeniable, and Ms. Miller knew she was falling in love.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Menza

item.title: From Kissing One Guy in 20 Years to 11 in a Month
item.description: My husband leaving nearly broke me. I tried to put myself back together by dating for the first time in my life.
item.dc:creator: Erin Chack
item.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: Once a Soloist, a Ballerina Finds Her Partner for Life
item.description: Soon after they started dating nearly three years ago, Olivia MacKinnon, a dancer with New York City Ballet, discovered Bill Keenan’s genuine passion for ballet.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova

item.title: It Started with a Midnight Swim and a Kiss Under the Stars
item.description: Marian Lurio and Jonathan Nguyen met at a mutual friend’s wedding four years ago and felt an immediate attraction.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Goodman

item.title: ‘Sparks Flew’ When Two Crushes Reunited After Three Decades
item.description: Elizabeth Maresca and Nicholas Magarelli liked each other in high school, but they never dated. They reconnected in 2017. Both were divorced. Neither was looking for love.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Why Has ‘Christ Is King’ Become a Controversial Statement?
item.description: For some, the three words have become encoded with a message of hate.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Taylor Pemberton
item.media:credit: From left, Rebecca Noble for The New York Times; Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times; Anna Watts for The New York Times
item.media:description: Conservative influencers like Candace Owens and Nick Fuentes have helped lead the re-emergence of the phrase as a politicized statement. Senator Ted Cruz has said he is troubled by its use on the far right.

item.title: How We Cover the White House Correspondents’ Dinner
item.description: The annual black-tie event attracts Hollywood stars, reporters and politicians for an irreverent night celebrating press freedom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: Politicians, Hollywood stars and journalists representing more than 100 news organizations attend the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington. Above, the scene in 2024.

item.title: New Phishing Scam: Fake Invitations
item.description: Hackers are spoofing Paperless Post, Evite and Punchbowl to creep into your hard drive.
item.dc:creator: Steven Kurutz
item.media:credit: Jack Nisberg/Condé Nast, via Getty Images
item.media:description: That party you were invited to? It may not be real.

item.title: My Adult Twins Fight Constantly. How Do I Stay Out of It?
item.description: Our Ask the Therapist columnist, Lori Gottlieb, advises a reader who wants to stop playing referee.
item.dc:creator: Lori Gottlieb
item.media:credit: Marta Monteiro

item.title: The Nerve Center of This Art Fair Isn’t Painting. It’s Couture.
item.description: The Independent fair will push boundaries, featuring designs by Rei Kawakubo and Comme des Garçons that blur the line between fashion and sculpture.
item.dc:creator: Tim F. Schneider

item.title: The Good List: 6 Things to Add Delight to Your Day
item.description: Mapping happiness, Monday dinners and the rise of the scapebear.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Tiny Love Stories: ‘Even Our Waitress Was Bored’
item.description: Modern Love in miniature, featuring reader-submitted stories of no more than 100 words.
item.media:credit: Brian Rea

item.title: My Parents Are Obsessed With Food and Weight. Help!
item.description: A reader fears that her parents’ decades-long food fixation could jeopardize her efforts to keep her own eating disorder under control.
item.dc:creator: Philip Galanes
item.media:credit: Miguel Porlan

item.title: Aging in Place: How Technology Might Help You Grow Old at Home
item.description: The budding field is turning dreams into reality for older adults who are eager to age in place, filling caregiving gaps and easing minds as America ages rapidly.
item.dc:creator: Susan Shain

item.title: Street Style Look of the Week: Airy Beachy Clothes
item.description: A couple stood out in Upper Manhattan for wearing billowy attire the colors of water and sand.
item.dc:creator: Simbarashe Cha

item.title: Met Gala 2026 Guide: Theme, Hosts, Dress Code and More
item.description: What to know about this year’s celebration, including the return of a superstar last seen on the Met carpet 10 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Damon Winter/The New York Times
item.media:description: Beyoncé arriving to the Met Gala in 2016, the last year she attended the event.

item.title: His Watch Brand Is ‘Doing It The Traditional Way’
item.description: Love Hunter has been drawn to timepieces since he was a child. Now, after years of selling and repairing them, it’s time for his own creations to take center stage.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Felder and Tony Cenicola
item.category: Smith, Alfred E, Career and Technical Education High School
item.media:description: “This was always the goal, to create something with my own hands,” said Love Hunter, with his son Mick, at his watchmaking shop in Derby, Conn.

item.title: With These Watches, It’s Not Just What’s on the Inside That Counts
item.description: A look at the new skeleton watch models unveiled by Hermès, Piaget and Panerai.
item.dc:creator: David Belcher

item.title: They Put Their Tool Watches to the Test
item.description: For these wearers of rugged timepieces, form follows function.
item.dc:creator: Penelope Colston
item.media:credit: Morten Wanvik for The New York Times
item.media:description: Morten Paulsen during transit aboard the anchor handler Island Valiant, wearing the Marathon Jumbo Diver’s Quartz JSAR 46mm, the watch he said he relied on most during offshore operations.

item.title: Luxury Brands Bet on the Middle East. War Has Damaged Their Plans.
item.description: High-end brands such as Louis Vuitton and Hermès are looking to other regions as sales in Persian Gulf nations plummet.
item.dc:creator: Kim Bhasin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
LVMH Moet Hennessy Louis Vuitton SA
item.media:credit: Benoit Tessier/Reuters
item.media:description: A Louis Vuitton store in Paris. LVMH has seen a drop in sales in its Middle Eastern stores, with demand falling across the gulf region.

item.title: What ‘Summer House’ Reveals About the Fallout From a Friend Dating Your Ex
item.description: From reality TV to real life, the emotional fallout of a friend dating an ex can last long after a relationship ends.
item.dc:creator: Martine Thompson

item.title: This Design Duo Loves a Challenge
item.description: When tastemakers are having a décor emergency, they bring in Adam Charlap Hyman and Andre Herrero.
item.dc:creator: Guy Trebay
item.media:credit: Evelyn Freja for The New York Times
item.media:description: Andre Herrero, left, and Adam Charlap Hyman are the latest winners of the annual Interior Design award overseen by the Cooper Hewitt Smithsonian Design Museum.

item.title: The Latest Trend in Wedding Fashion: The Bouquet ‘Purse’
item.description: Once popular in the Victorian era, flower “purses,” crafted of faux and real flowers, are being carried down the aisle with a handle or grasped like a clutch.
item.dc:creator: Perri Ormont Blumberg

item.title: Tackling the Trust Factor for Secondhand Watches
item.description: Online start-ups aim to clarify pricing and authenticity for pre-owned and vintage watches.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Gomelsky
item.media:credit: Beth Coller for The New York Times
item.media:description: Quaid Walker, chief executive of Bezel, an online marketplace for pre-owned luxury watches.

item.title: For the British Watch Brand Bremont, the Next Stop Is the Moon
item.description: The billionaire Bill Ackman’s investment in the company might not yet have vastly altered its fortunes, but he’s concerned with more than earthly riches.
item.dc:creator: Robin Swithinbank

item.title: With Every Watch, This Collector Revels in the Story
item.description: Jay Liu shares some of his favorite spots in Tokyo, along with his reasons for collecting vintage timepieces.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Morelli and James Whitlow Delano
item.media:description: The watch collector Jay Liu wearing a vintage Rolex Daytona at the Spiral building in Tokyo.

item.title: Chanel Takes a Leading Role in Producing Ceramic Watches
item.description: The fashion house has expanded its signature line, the J12, which is now offered in three colors and new sizes.
item.dc:creator: Ming Liu
item.media:credit: Joann Pai for The New York Times
item.media:description: Frédéric Grangié, president of Chanel watches and fine jewelry. “By watchmaker standards, we are very, very young,” he said. “But if I look at the number of creations, novelties and unexpected designs that we brought over the course of 40 years, it is probably more than most.”
